target.name: Transmutex
target.founder: Franklin Servan-Schreiber, Federico Carminati, Jean-Pierre Revol

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Transmutex, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Servan-Schreiber, Carminati, and Revol, has raised $23M to date. The nuclear energy sector comp universe includes 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Oklo.

Only one valuation method is reliably applicable. The stage-progression approach, using the $23M raised as a proxy for post-money value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yields an implied valuation of $46M. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable due to insufficient comparable data within the narrow Series A nuclear energy cohort; while 8 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three meet same-stage and similar-tier criteria simultaneously, making sector-wide medians unreliably loose.

The triangulated valuation range is $39M (low) to $53M (high), with a median of $46M. This single-method result reflects meaningful uncertainty around whether Transmutex's technology maturity, intellectual property scope, and capital requirements align with historical Series A progression in the nuclear sector. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway clarity, technical feasibility validation milestones, and the company's burn rate relative to $23M deployed capital. Further diligence on management execution and comparable funding rounds within the advanced nuclear subsector would strengthen conviction in this range.
